Overview.content: Persistence operates in two tiers: an in-memory tier (session-scoped, low-latency, used for live queries) and a JPA/PostgreSQL tier (durable, artifact-scoped, used for long-term storage and replay). The in-memory tier drives all real-time operations — SSE replay, interrupt lookups, graph traversal. The JPA tier kicks in only when an execution completes and the artifact tree is flushed.

Two-Tier Architecture.content:
```
In-Memory Tier (session-scoped)                    JPA/PostgreSQL Tier (durable)
─────────────────────────────────                   ────────────────────────────
InMemoryGraphRepository                             ArtifactEntity (table: artifact)
  ConcurrentHashMap<nodeId, GraphNode>                artifactKey, parentKey, executionKey
  findSubtree(), findInterruptByOrigin()              contentHash (SHA-256), contentJson (TEXT)
                                                      depth, templateStaticId, shared, childIds
InMemoryEventStreamRepository
  ConcurrentHashMap<nodeId, List<GraphEvent>>       SemanticRepresentationEntity
  + byId map for direct lookup                        targetArtifactKey, embeddings (binary)
  ArtifactKey.isDescendantOf() for scoped queries     summaries (JSON), classifications

InMemoryWorktreeRepository                          ArtifactRepository (Spring Data JPA + QueryDSL)
InMemoryRequestContextRepository                      findByContentHash(), findByKeyPrefix()
InMemoryRunPersistenceCheckRepository                 findByExecutionKeyAndArtifactType()
                                                      findAllExecutions(), findExecutionKeysBetween()
```

Artifact Persistence Flow.content:
```
Agent emits artifact (ArtifactEmissionService)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactEventListener.onEvent()
 │
 ├─ ArtifactEvent → walks tree by depth, inserts into in-memory ArtifactTreeBuilder
 ├─ Semantic graph events → mapped to EventArtifact via EventArtifactMapper
 └─ Stream events → mapped to MessageStreamArtifact
 │
 │  (artifacts may arrive before their parent — queued in pendingArtifactsByExecution)
 │
 ▼
ExecutionScopeService.completeExecution(workflowRunId, status)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactEventListener.finishPersistRemove(executionKey)
 │
 ▼
ArtifactService.doPersist()
 ├─ Collects all artifacts from the tree
 ├─ Groups by ArtifactKey (handles key collisions by creating child keys)
 ├─ Groups by contentHash:
 │    ├─ Hash exists in DB → creates DbRef (reference artifact, no payload duplication)
 │    └─ Templated content → creates TemplateDbRef instead of ArtifactDbRef
 ├─ Saves via ArtifactRepository (JPA)
 └─ Errors publish NodeErrorEvent to EventBus
```

ArtifactKey: The Hierarchical Identifier.content: `ArtifactKey` is a hierarchical `ak:ulid/ulid/...` string. Parent-child relationships are encoded in the key structure itself — `ArtifactKey.isDescendantOf()` checks if one key is a prefix of another. This enables efficient scoped queries without joins: all events/interrupts/permissions under a workflow run share a common key prefix.

SemanticRepresentation: Post-Hoc Enrichm.content: `SemanticRepresentationEntity` stores post-hoc semantic interpretations (embeddings, summaries, classifications, named entities) keyed by `targetArtifactKey`. It never mutates the source artifact — it's a separate table that enriches artifacts after the fact.

Direct Persistence Bypass.content: If no execution root is present in memory but the artifact's parent exists in the persistent store (`artifactService.existsByArtifactKey()`), the artifact is persisted directly without being added to the in-memory tree. This prevents stale in-memory state from blocking persistence of late-arriving artifacts.
